name: workout-dumbbell-holder
description: Internal-plug + external-sleeve clip-on cradle that mounts a rubber-coated dumbbell vertically (along the rail axis) on a treadmill rectangular extrusion. v3 adds an external sleeve for dual-engagement and lowers the fork ~20 mm below the rail-top face to reduce stress at the plug root.

notes.dimensions: v3 bbox. x = plug_wide (68.5) + 2×flange_overhang (10) = 88.5 mm. y = flange_y_back (31.25) + cradle_reach (90) + tine_above (20) = 141.25 mm. z = plug_depth (30) + flange_thickness (8) = 38 mm.
notes.orientationAssumption: Plug axis = Z (rail axis). Shaft direction = Z (parallel to rail — dumbbell hangs along the extrusion). Cradle extends in +Y direction (perpendicular to plug axis). Saddle profile is in XY plane (horizontal), shaft passes through slot vertically.
notes.v1_v2_history: v1 had shaft perpendicular to rail (drop-in vertical fork). v2 corrected to shaft parallel to rail but kept the plate above the flange. v3 adds sleeve + lowers fork below flange. See requirements.md v3 amendment for full rationale.

tolerances.note: 1.0 mm/side user-specified clearance on both plug axes (generous sliding fit). CF-reinforced filament may shrink less than PLA — test pocket print required before full part. Project standard sliding fit is 0.35 mm/side; this is intentionally larger.

volume.note: Estimated range for a solid-shelled CF part with 3 mm minimum walls. Plug body + flange + cradle stem + two tines. Exact value depends on final tine wall thickness and infill — confirm after modeling.

params.sleeve_length_was_30: extended +18mm in v3.3 to make room for the symmetric bottom buttress
params.sleeve_minus_x_wall_removed_reason: Treadmill control panel structure abuts the rail on the -X side per user-supplied reference photo. The sleeve's -X short wall and two -X corners are cut so the holder clears the console.
params.fork_top_buttress_note: r=22 quarter-cylinder gusset filling air pocket between fork-top (Z=-22) and flange-top edge (Z=0). Tangent points (Y=+31.25, Z=0) and (Y=+53.25, Z=-22).
params.fork_bottom_buttress_note: Mirror of top buttress reflected about Z=-28 (mid-fork). Tangent at (Y=+31.25, Z=-56) on new sleeve-bottom edge and (Y=+53.25, Z=-34) on fork bottom. Same X range as top buttress.
params.fork_shrunk_minus_x: fork plate cut at X=[-44.25, -39.25] for Y=[+31.25, +110], Z=[-34, -22] so it no longer overhangs the buttress on the -X side

fork_bottom_ribs.purpose: add bending-stiffness section depth to each tine; inner ends join sleeve +Y wall, outer ends are free at 5mm before tine tip

params.bore_clear: confirmed — open end, no internal obstruction
params.plug_depth_note: 30 mm engagement, matched by sleeve_length 30 mm on the outside. v3 dual-engagement (plug + sleeve in opposition) approximately doubles the effective rail-holder reaction couple lever arm vs v1/v2 plug-alone.
params.v3_local_beefup_note: At fork Z range (saddle centerline ± fork_thickness_z/2), the +Y sleeve wall is replaced by the fork plate base extending from Y=+26.5 (sleeve inner +Y face) outward to Y=+110 (tine tips). This locally thickens the sleeve at the fork junction without adding material elsewhere.

testPrintCandidates.reason: 1.0 mm/side clearance was chosen to be conservative for CF-reinforced filament, which shrinks less than PLA. The project tolerance table is calibrated for plain PLA. A short plug test pocket (30 mm deep, full cross-section) should be printed and test-fitted in the extrusion bore before the full part is committed. If it fits with acceptable slop, proceed; if it binds, the clearance needs to increase.

testPrintCandidates.feature: Plug wall minimum cross-section at cantilever root
testPrintCandidates.reason: The bending moment is highest at the plug-to-flange transition. CF-reinforced filament has lower impact toughness and potentially weaker inter-layer adhesion than unfilled PLA. The engagement depth has been reduced to 30 mm (from an initial 80 mm proposal), shortening the lever arm that resists the cantilever moment. A structural section test (short plug stub with flange) should be load-tested by hand before the full part is printed.
